Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current grid from segmentation:
# N g N  (top)
# N g N  (mid)
# N g N  (bot)
# 
# Target (plus pattern from purple dots):
# g N g  (top)
# N g N  (mid)
# g N g  (bot)
#
# Need to toggle: top-left, top-center, top-right, bot-left, bot-center, bot-right

# Click on top row and bottom row cells
result = action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 25},  # top-left: N → g
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30},  # top-center: g → N
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 35},  # top-right: N → g
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 25},  # bot-left: N → g
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30},  # bot-center: g → N
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 35},  # bot-right: N → g
])
print(f"After painting: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Verify grid state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    r_min, c_min = min(rows), min(cols)
    if 47 <= r_min <= 62 and 22 <= c_min <= 36 and n['pixels'] >= 9:
        print(f"  Cell at ({r_min}, {c_min}): color={n['color']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
